description: Apply Bayes' formula to update a prior probability to a posterior probability in the light of new evidence — the formal rule for rational belief revision

fields.name: prior_probability
fields.label: Prior probability P(event) before observing evidence

fields.name: complementary_likelihood
fields.label: P(evidence | not event) — used with prior to derive denominator

fields.name: unconditional_evidence
fields.label: P(evidence) directly, if known — else derived via total probability

fields.name: multiple_scenarios
fields.label: Array of {scenario_id, prior, likelihood} for multi-hypothesis Bayes

core_formula.bayes_two_scenario: P(event | evidence) = P(evidence | event) * P(event) / P(evidence)
core_formula.denominator_total_probability: P(evidence) = P(evidence | event) * P(event) + P(evidence | not event) * P(not event)
core_formula.bayes_multi_scenario: P(S_i | evidence) = P(evidence | S_i) * P(S_i) / sum_j[ P(evidence | S_j) * P(S_j) ]

interpretation.likelihood: Probability of observing the evidence under each hypothesis
interpretation.evidence_informativeness: Evidence shifts prior more when likelihoods differ substantially across hypotheses

key_properties.inversion: Bayes swaps the direction of conditioning: P(A|B) → P(B|A)
key_properties.sequential_updates: Today's posterior becomes tomorrow's prior; natural fit for streaming evidence
key_properties.calibration: Formula is correct; the art is in specifying priors and likelihoods

common_fallacies.base_rate_neglect: Ignoring the prior → confusing P(evidence | event) with P(event | evidence)
common_fallacies.prosecutor_fallacy: Treating P(evidence | innocent) as P(innocent | evidence)

investment_applications.earnings_surprise: Update P(firm_high_quality) given positive earnings surprise
investment_applications.credit_analysis: Update default probability given rating migration
investment_applications.technical_signals: Update P(trend_up) given breakout, factoring false-positive rate
investment_applications.portfolio_managers: Bayesian blending of prior and observed returns (Black-Litterman)

compute_two_scenario.description: Standard Bayes with event and complement

errors.message: P(evidence) is zero; posterior probability is undefined
Prior probability and likelihood are required

anti_patterns.rule: Do not commit the base-rate fallacy by ignoring the prior
anti_patterns.why: A test with 99% sensitivity for a 1-in-10000 disease still produces mostly false positives; ignoring the prior yields wildly wrong posteriors

anti_patterns.rule: Do not confuse P(evidence | hypothesis) with P(hypothesis | evidence)
anti_patterns.why: The prosecutor's fallacy; Bayes exists precisely because these are different quantities that can differ by orders of magnitude

anti_patterns.rule: Do not apply Bayes with a prior that ignores available domain information
anti_patterns.why: Uniform priors over vast hypothesis spaces are often worse than weakly informative priors; garbage-in, garbage-out still applies

anti_patterns.rule: Do not perform sequential updates using the same evidence twice
anti_patterns.why: Each piece of evidence must be independent conditional on the hypothesis; reusing evidence produces overconfident posteriors

goals.description: Apply Bayes' formula to update a prior probability to a posterior probability in the light of new evidence — the formal rule for rational belief revision

success_metrics.measurement: All decisions have complete audit trails

constraints.description: All operations must be auditable and traceable

tradeoffs.reason: trading operations require precise execution and full audit trails

invariants: error messages never expose internal system details

worked_example.scenario: Prior P(high_quality) = 0.7; P(positive_surprise | high_quality) = 0.45; P(positive_surprise | low_quality) = 0.10
worked_example.interpretation: Positive surprise raises conviction from 70% to 91.3%

diagnostic_test_example.scenario: Disease prevalence 0.01; test sensitivity 0.99; test specificity 0.95
diagnostic_test_example.interpretation: Positive test gives only 16.7% probability of disease due to low prior
